summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering cleanup day planning, zoning refunds, solar energy restrictions discussion with County Commissioner Painter, personnel actions, and the Combs dumps remediation. The board went into executive session twice—once for legal matters with the prosecutor and once for personnel interviews. Key decisions included promoting a service foreman, appointing a zoning commission alternate, and requesting Land Bank intervention for the Combs dumps properties.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (April 11–24, 2023) passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:07 PM for legal conference passed
Motion to set cleanup day for June 3, 2023, 8 AM to noon at Station 18 passed
Motion to refund $200 to Grayson Motorsports from zoning account 2181 passed
Motion to refund $200 to Glenn Grooms from zoning account 2181 passed
Motion to close bicentennial account (5553) and move funds to primary account (3054) passed
Motion to accept resignation of Tyler Read effective April 2023 passed
Motion to approve service agreement with iWorQ for zoning software passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:38 PM for personnel matters passed
Motion to promote Joe Reynolds to service foreman effective May 7, 2023 at $1 above highest paid service worker with one-year probationary period passed
Motion to appoint Steve Pegram as alternate on zoning commission with term expiring April 25, 2025 passed
Motion to advertise for BZA members passed
Motion to request Clermont County Land Bank intervention with Combs dumps and for county treasurer to seek relief from bankruptcy stay passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:54 PM passed

notable_topics: Cleanup day planning for June 3 at Station 18 with traffic management concerns
Zoning fee refunds for sign changes that should not have been charged
Closure of dormant bicentennial bank account
Adoption of iWorQ zoning software for permits and property maintenance tracking
Senate Bill 52 solar energy restrictions discussion with Commissioner Painter
Solar farm restriction options for townships (large-scale 50kW+ and community-scale 5-49.5MW)
Promotion of Joe Reynolds to service foreman
Appointment of Steve Pegram as zoning commission alternate
Combs dumps remediation and Land Bank intervention request
Tornado recovery update including firehouse and building roof repairs

State property re-evaluation concerns (43% increase proposed)
notes: Meeting included two executive sessions: one with prosecutor Brian Tribe regarding pending/imminent court action, and one for personnel interview. The May 9 meeting will be cancelled as two trustees will be unavailable; next meeting scheduled for May 23. Commissioner Painter provided extensive informal briefing on Senate Bill 52 solar restrictions - 7 of 14 Clermont County townships have already submitted restriction resolutions. The board discussed that Goshen Township's higher property values and limited large agricultural parcels make large-scale solar less likely but community-scale projects (5-49.5MW under House Bill 401) may be more relevant. OTA scholarship congratulations card read from Nathan Paparocki.
title: BOT 4.25.23
